nodes.name: Extract important data

values.name: linkedin_posts
values.arrayValue: ={{ $input.item.json.data.slice(0, 10).map(d => { return { text: d.text, likes: d.num_likes, comments: d.num_comments, postedAt: d.posted } } ) }}

nodes.notes: This set node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Extract important data again

values.name: twitter_posts
values.arrayValue: ={{ $input.item.json.results.map(d => { return { text: d.text, favorites: d.favorite_count, retweets: d.retweet_count, replies: d.reply_count, postedAt: d.creationDate} }) }}

nodes.notes: This set node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Ask AI to summerize

messages.content:
=I am a sales representative in my company and I want to see social media activity for a company I am about to meet. I will give you a JSON object  containing company name, and lists of recent LinkedIn and X(Twitter) posts and your job is to give me a summary these posts. This summary needs to be in textual form suitable for email (just the content without the subject and should be impersonal) and should include details interested to a sales representative such as me. JSON data has the following properties: - 'name': Company name, - 'linkedin_posts': List of LinkedIn posts, - 'twitter_posts': List of Twitter posts, You can ignore all other properties in the JSON data. This is the data:
{{ JSON.stringify($json) }}

nodes.notes: This openAi node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: google Calendar  O Auth2  A P I details
open Ai  A P I details
configurationSteps: Open the scheduler node "Every morning @ 7".
Adjust the execution intervals (e.g., Every hour, Daily, or Custom CRON expression) according to your preferences.
Open the "Get meetings for today" node (n8n-nodes-base.googleCalendar) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Split Out" node (n8n-nodes-base.splitOut) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get recent LinkedIn posts" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Enrich attendee company" node (n8n-nodes-base.clearbit) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Gmail" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmail) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Combine all activity for a company" node (n8n-nodes-base.merge) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Prepare email template" node (n8n-nodes-base.html) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Keep only ones with the domain" node (n8n-nodes-base.filter) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the AI node "Ask AI to summerize".
Adjust the Model settings (e.g. gpt-4o, gpt-3.5-turbo) and write or refine the System Prompt parameters.
Open the "Switch" node (n8n-nodes-base.switch) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Wrap everything together" node (n8n-nodes-base.merge) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get latest tweets" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Setup".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Get attendees email domains".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Extract data for email".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Extract important data".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Extract important data again".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
